channel.title: Nikhil Rai — Blog
channel.description: Notes and insights on strategic partnerships, business development, lead generation, automation and growth.

item.title: B2B Lead Generation Strategy: From Cold List to Predictable Pipeline
item.description: A B2B lead generation strategy is the plan that makes pipeline predictable, not the tactics — the ICP, offer, channel and qualification that make lead generation compound.

item.title: Growth Operations: The System That Makes Growth Repeatable
item.description: Marketing and sales create motion; growth operations makes it repeatable. What growth ops is, why it's the function nobody owns, and how to build it without hiring a team.

item.title: Types of Strategic Partnerships (and How to Choose the Right One)
item.description: "Partnership" isn't one thing. Channel, technology, content, referral and co-marketing partnerships — what each is actually good for, and how to choose the right type for your goal.

item.title: Signs Your Operations Are Broken (and How to Fix Them)
item.description: Broken operations rarely announce themselves — they show up as heroics, dropped balls and slow decisions. The signs your operations are broken, and how to fix the causes.

item.title: Product-Led vs Sales-Led Growth: Which Is Right for You?
item.description: Product-led vs sales-led growth isn't about which is trendier — it's about your price, product and buyer. How to tell which motion fits, and when to blend both.

item.title: Cold Outreach That Actually Gets Replies
item.description: Most cold outreach fails because it's about the sender, not the recipient. A practical framework for cold email and outreach that earns replies without spraying volume.

item.title: Lead Generation vs Demand Generation: What's the Difference?
item.description: Demand generation creates demand; lead generation captures it. The real difference between the two, why you need both, and how to sequence them so pipeline compounds.

item.title: How Much Should You Spend on Automation?
item.description: How much to spend on automation isn't a budget question — it's an ROI question. A simple framework to size automation spend by what each task really costs you.

item.title: The Partnership Agreement Checklist: What to Nail Down Before You Sign
item.description: Most partnerships fail on the things nobody agreed up front. A practical partnership agreement checklist: the goals, ownership, economics and exit terms to settle before you sign.

item.title: Referrals Don't Happen by Accident
item.description: Word of mouth is your highest-trust, lowest-cost channel — and most teams leave it to luck. How to turn referrals into a system you design, ask for and reward, instead of hoping happy customers remember you.

item.title: You Don't Have a Growth Problem — You Have a Positioning Problem
item.description: Most "growth problems" are positioning problems in disguise. Why clarity about who you're for and what you replace beats more spend, more channels and more features — and how to fix it.

item.title: Retention Is the Growth You Already Paid For
item.description: Acquisition gets the budget, but retention compounds. Why keeping and expanding the customers you already won out-grows chasing new ones — and the activation, expansion and rescue habits that make it happen.

item.title: Distribution Is the Product
item.description: A great product doesn't market itself. Distribution is a discipline you design — choosing one channel, earning it, and making it compound — not an afterthought you bolt on at launch.

item.title: Go-to-Market From Zero: Your First 100 Users
item.description: How to launch when nobody knows you exist — pick a beachhead, go where your first users already gather, do the unscalable work, and turn early momentum into growth.

item.title: Your Lead Problem Isn't Volume — It's Signal
item.description: Most teams that think they need more leads actually need better signal. How to read buying intent and route effort to the leads that convert.

item.title: Good Operations Is Invisible
item.description: You notice operations only when they're bad. The processes, ownership and reporting that let a team execute calmly and predictably.

item.title: Business Development Strategy: A System, Not a Rolodex
item.description: BD isn't sales or networking — it's the disciplined work of finding and creating growth opportunities. How to run it as a repeatable system.

item.title: Growth Marketing Strategy Without the Hacks
item.description: Growth marketing isn't viral tricks — it's a disciplined system of experiments across the funnel. A framework for growth that compounds.

item.title: What to Automate First (and What to Leave Alone)
item.description: Automation should buy back time, but most teams automate the wrong things first. A simple way to decide what to automate, fix, or leave to people.

item.title: How to Build Strategic Partnerships That Actually Drive Growth
item.description: Most partnerships stall after the announcement. A practical framework for building ones that create real, lasting growth.

item.title: Building a Lead-Gen System That Runs Without You
item.description: Turn sporadic outreach into a predictable system — the components that make pipeline repeatable instead of reliant on hustle.
